definition: Rejects/challenges claim primarily via personal attack on actor rather than claim merits.
notes: Multi-label fallacy (§5.2). Max 3 fallacy labels per unit; pick top 3 by salience. Label only if move present in span (§6 rule 1). Context window: ±1 adjacent unit from same document_id (§6 rule 2).

definition: Misrepresents opponent position (stronger/weaker) then refutes the distortion.
notes: Multi-label fallacy (§5.2). Max 3 fallacy labels per unit. Do not infer intent beyond text + minimal context (§5.2).

definition: Presents two (or few) exclusive options when reasonable alternatives exist.
notes: Multi-label fallacy (§5.2). Max 3 fallacy labels per unit.

definition: Claims action inevitably leads to extreme outcome without sufficient evidential steps.
notes: Multi-label fallacy (§5.2). Max 3 fallacy labels per unit.

definition: Replaces/evades evidentiary support with fear, outrage, or pity as primary warrant.
notes: Multi-label fallacy (§5.2). Max 3 fallacy labels per unit.

definition: Generalizes from unrepresentative or insufficient evidence.
notes: Multi-label fallacy (§5.2). Max 3 fallacy labels per unit.

definition: Deflects criticism by pointing to opponent's unrelated or symmetric behavior without addressing merit.
notes: Multi-label fallacy (§5.2). Max 3 fallacy labels per unit.

definition: No fallacy present (store as empty list + fallacy_none_explicit=true).
notes: Not combined with other fallacy labels (§5.2). When selected, export fallacy_labels as empty list with fallacy_none_explicit=true (§4.3).
